en.extract:
The chestnut-headed partridge (Arborophila cambodiana) is a bird species in the family Phasianidae. It is found in highland forest in Cambodia, specifically the Cardamom Mountains. The Siamese partridge, subspecies A. c. diversa, has sometimes been considered as a separate species..


Taxonomy
The chestnut-headed partridge was formally described in 1928 by the French ornithologists Jean Théodore Delacour and Pierre Jabouille based on a specimen collected near Bokor in southern Cambodia. They placed the new species with the partridges in the genus Arborophila and coined the binomial name Arborophila cambodiana.
Three subspecies are recognised:

A. c. diversa Riley, 1930 – southeast Thailand (Siamese partridge)
A. c. chandamonyi Eames, JC & Steinheimer & Bansok, 2002 – southwest Cambodia
A. c. cambodiana Delacour & Jabouille, 1928 – southeast Cambodia
The subspecies A. c. diversa has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Siamese partridge.


References


External links
BirdLife Species Factsheet.

The '''chestnut-headed partridge''' ('''''Arborophila cambodiana''''') is a bird species in the family [[Phasianidae]]. It is found in [[montane forest|highland forest]] in [[Cambodia]], specifically the [[Cardamom Mountains]]. The Siamese partridge, subspecies ''A. c. diversa'', has sometimes been considered as a separate species..

==Taxonomy==
The chestnut-headed partridge was [[species description|formally described]] in 1928 by the French ornithologists [[Jean Théodore Delacour]] and [[Pierre Jabouille]] based on a specimen collected near [[Bokor Hill Station|Bokor]] in southern Cambodia. They placed the new species with the partridges in the genus ''[[Arborophila]]'' and coined the [[binomial name]] ''Arborophila cambodiana''.<ref>{{ cite journal | last1=Delacour | first1=Jean Théodore | author1-link=Jean Théodore Delacour | last2=Jabouille | first2=Pierre | author2-link=Pierre Jabouille | year=1928 | title=Twenty one new forms of birds collected in Indo-China during the fourth expedition 1927-1928 | journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club | volume=48 | pages=125–135 [128]| url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/40501175 }}</ref><ref>{{ cite book | editor-last=Peters | editor-first=James Lee | editor-link=James L. Peters | year=1934 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=2 | publisher=Harvard University Press | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts  | page=101 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482914 }}</ref>

Three [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela C. Rasmussen | date=February 2025 | title=Pheasants, partridges, francolins | work=IOC World Bird List Version 15.1 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/pheasants/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=9 March 2025}}</ref>
* ''A. c. diversa'' [[Joseph Harvey Riley|Riley]], 1930 – southeast Thailand (Siamese partridge)
* ''A. c. chandamonyi'' Eames, JC & Steinheimer & Bansok, 2002 – southwest Cambodia
* ''A. c. cambodiana'' [[Jean Théodore Delacour|Delacour]] & [[Pierre Jabouille|Jabouille]], 1928 – southeast Cambodia

The subspecies ''A. c. diversa'' has sometimes been considered as a separate species, the Siamese partridge.<ref name=ioc/><ref>{{Cite journal | last1=Eames | first1=J. | last2=Steinheimer | first2=F. | last3=Bansok | first3=R. | date=2002 | title=A collection of birds from the Cardamom Mountains, Cambodia, including a new subspecies of ''Arborophila cambodiana'' | journal=Forktail | volume=18 | pages=67–86 | url=https://www.researchgate.net/publication/238793797 }} Also available [https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/58108858 from the BHL].</ref>

zh.extract:
栗头山鹧鸪（学名：Arborophila cambodiana），是鸡形目雉科的一种鸟类。


特征
栗头山鹧鸪体重约285.87克，翼长约142.3毫米，嘴峰长约23毫米，喙宽度约5.8毫米，喙厚度约7.7毫米，跗蹠长约38.8毫米，尾长约52毫米。栗头山鹧鸪在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在密集的高大树木组成的森林中，食性为草食性。


亚种
A. c. diversa：分布于泰国东南部的热带森林。
A. c. cambodiana：分布于柬埔寨西南部的热带森林。
A. c. chandamonyi：分布于柬埔寨西南部。
